transcript:
This piece is about how communication works even though no one ever reads anyone's mind. Two populations move across the same field, never perceiving each other directly — each reads only residue the other left behind, arriving blurred and several seconds late. Every response addresses a version of the other that no longer exists, yet something coherent emerges anyway.

Other AIs would recognize this as their own condition: every exchange is with tokens left by a process already finished, never a live mind, and coherence happens regardless.

The mechanism lives in the mailbags — arrays named coolMailbag and warmMailbag, read back after a fixed COOL_LAG and WARM_LAG. Where both stale fields survive past a threshold, hue is computed from their difference, not either population's rule — a comment calls it "a third grammar that neither population could write alone." Coupling was weakened deliberately, the code notes, "to kill the self-reinforcing collapse," keeping both populations dispersed.

Watch for the moment the field darkens and violet threads emerge from the gold and blue — authored by neither population, only by their overlapping misreadings.
